name: Washington

examFacts.notes: Administered by PSI for the Washington Real Estate Commission and the Department of Licensing (DOL). The 130 scored questions split into a 100-question national portion (150 minutes) and a 30-question Washington portion (90 minutes), each scored separately at a scaled 70 pass mark.

intro:
In Washington the entry-level credential is the broker license, not salesperson, and on that exam the separately scored 30-question state section is where first-timers come up short. The Washington State Department of Licensing delivers it through PSI as two separate portions, a 100-question national section over two and a half hours and a 30-question Washington section over ninety minutes, for 130 scored questions in all. You need a scaled score of 70 on each portion and the two are scored separately, so a strong national score won't rescue a weak state score. That gap on the Washington section is why Washington-specific preparation matters more here than another round of national review.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. Washington runs on its own rules, including the license law in RCW 18.85, the brokerage relationships act in RCW 18.86 that sets each agent's statutory duties, the Form 17 seller disclosure statement, trust account and earnest money handling by the designated broker, the real estate excise tax, and the community property rules that decide how married couples hold and convey title. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need Washington-specific practice questions.

Below are 20 free Washington real estate test questions and answers from RealReady's full Washington bank. Each tests a specific topic with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation under the card. Read each one, commit to an answer, then reveal. Treat it like a real PSI practice test, and the ones you miss show you what to focus on next.

Get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android for the rest of the Washington question bank. The full app includes short articles that walk you through the why behind each topic, detailed explanations on every question, a missed-question drill mode, and progress tracking that shows your per-category accuracy.

faq.q: How many questions are on the Washington real estate exam?
faq.a: The Washington Real Estate Broker Examination has 130 scored questions, split into a 100-question national portion and a 30-question Washington state portion. PSI also mixes in a small number of unscored pretest questions, usually 5 to 10, that look just like the real ones and do not count toward your score. The two portions are scored separately, so you have to pass both.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the Washington real estate exam?
faq.a: You need a scaled score of 70 on each portion, and the two are scored separately. PSI reports a scaled score rather than a raw percentage, so the exact number of questions you must answer correctly can shift a little from one form of the test to the next, but the bar sits at 70 on both the national and Washington portions. You can pass one and fail the other, and you only have to retake the portion you missed.

faq.q: How much does the Washington real estate exam cost?
faq.a: PSI charges $210 to sit the broker exam, and that single fee covers both the national and Washington portions. You pay PSI when you schedule, and the fee is not refundable or transferable. If you fail one portion, you only have to repeat that portion, but you re-register and pay again to take it.

faq.q: How long is the Washington real estate exam?
faq.a: Four hours of testing time in total. PSI gives you two and a half hours for the 100-question national portion and ninety minutes for the 30-question Washington portion. You take both portions at a PSI test center, and your unofficial result is available as soon as you finish.

faq.q: Who administers the Washington real estate exam?
faq.a: The Washington Real Estate Broker Examination is administered by PSI for the Washington Real Estate Commission and the Department of Licensing (DOL). You register and schedule your exam date through PSI.

faq.q: Is the Washington real estate exam hard?
faq.a: The national portion is the same caliber you would find on any PSI real estate exam, and plenty of first-time candidates underestimate it. The Washington portion is shorter but unforgiving, since 30 questions leave little room for error and the material comes straight from Washington statute and rule. Most people who struggle spent their study time on national principles and skimped on Washington's agency law, disclosure rules, and trust account handling. Honest practice on a Washington-specific practice test is the difference between passing on the first try and paying to retake a portion.

faq.q: What's on the Washington real estate exam?
faq.a: The 100-question national portion covers general real estate principles, including property ownership, land use controls, valuation, financing, agency, property disclosures, contracts, transfer of title, the practice of real estate, and real estate math, with contracts and agency carrying the most weight. The 30-question Washington portion leans hardest on the statutes and rules that govern licensee activity, things like trust accounts and the handling of money, prohibited conduct, compensation, advertising, property management, and supervision. It also covers the powers of the Real Estate Commission and the Director, Washington licensing requirements, and the brokerage relationships and agency duties set out in RCW 18.86.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the Washington real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish your required pre-license coursework, the highest-leverage thing you can do is grind through practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. Generic real estate practice tests help with the national principles, but they will not catch you on Washington's statutory agency rules, disclosure requirements, or trust account handling. The RealReady app gives you the full Washington question bank plus the national bank, with progress tracking and a missed-question mode so you can drill what you are weak on. Most users study 30 to 60 minutes a day for two to four weeks before sitting the exam.

The 20 Washington practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the Washington bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the Washington real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated Washington question bank built for the Washington real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the Washington law that trips people up, like the license law in RCW 18.85, the brokerage relationships act in RCW 18.86, and the Form 17 seller disclosure statement.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward Washington's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
